collection: Science Fiction, Fantasy, Mystery, Thriller, Horror, Young Adult & Children

authors.name: Isaac Asimov
authors.biography: Russian-born American biochemist and one of science fiction's Big Three, author or editor of more than five hundred books spanning robots, galactic empires, and popular science.

books.title: Foundation
books.description: Psychohistorian Hari Seldon foresees the Galactic Empire's fall and seeds a Foundation to shorten the coming dark age from thirty millennia to one.

books.title: I, Robot
books.description: Nine linked stories introduce the Three Laws of Robotics and the tangled logic puzzles they create for robopsychologist Susan Calvin.

books.title: The Caves of Steel
books.description: Agoraphobic Earth detective Elijah Baley partners with humanoid robot R. Daneel Olivaw to solve a Spacer's murder in the enclosed cities of future Earth.

books.title: The Gods Themselves
books.description: A free-energy exchange with a parallel universe threatens both worlds, and only beings on each side who question it can act, in Asimov's Hugo and Nebula winner.

authors.name: Arthur C. Clarke
authors.biography: English futurist and science fiction grandmaster who proposed the communications satellite and co-created the most famous film in the genre.

books.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
books.description: From a bone tossed skyward to the enigmatic monolith orbiting Jupiter, humanity is shepherded toward transcendence — if HAL 9000 doesn't end the trip first.

books.title: Childhood's End
books.description: Benevolent alien Overlords end war and want on Earth, at the price of a destiny that no human parent can follow.

books.title: Rendezvous with Rama
books.description: A fifty-kilometer alien cylinder coasts through the solar system, and the crew of Endeavour has weeks to explore its silent, purposeful interior.

authors.name: Frank Herbert
authors.biography: American journalist and ecological visionary whose desert-planet epic became the bestselling science fiction novel of all time.

books.title: Dune
books.description: Young Paul Atreides survives betrayal on the desert planet Arrakis, source of the spice that binds an empire, and rises among the Fremen toward a terrible destiny.

books.title: Dune Messiah
books.description: Twelve years into Paul's jihad-stained reign, conspirators close in on the Emperor who can see the future but not escape it.

authors.name: Ursula K. Le Guin
authors.biography: American writer who brought anthropological depth and moral imagination to science fiction and fantasy, mapping anarchist moons and dragon-haunted archipelagos.

books.title: The Left Hand of Darkness
books.description: Envoy Genly Ai must win over the ambisexual people of ice-bound Gethen, where his only ally is a politician he cannot bring himself to trust.

books.title: The Dispossessed
books.description: Physicist Shevek travels from his anarchist moon to the capitalist mother planet to finish a theory that could unite the stars — or be owned by one world.

books.title: A Wizard of Earthsea
books.description: Proud young mage Ged unleashes a nameless shadow in a moment of arrogance and must pursue it to the edge of the world to learn its true name.

books.title: The Lathe of Heaven
books.description: George Orr's dreams literally rewrite reality, and the psychiatrist who controls them keeps improving the world into stranger catastrophes.

authors.name: Ray Bradbury
authors.biography: American fantasist of carnivals, rockets, and small-town Octobers, whose lyric short stories carried science fiction into the literary mainstream.

books.title: Fahrenheit 451
books.description: Fireman Guy Montag burns books for a living until a curious neighbor and a hidden Bible turn him against the state of enforced amusement.

books.title: The Martian Chronicles
books.description: Linked stories chart humanity's colonization of Mars — the ghosts displaced, the towns rebuilt, the Earth left burning behind.

books.title: Something Wicked This Way Comes
books.description: Cooger & Dark's Pandemonium Shadow Show rolls into Green Town offering everyone their dearest wish, and two boys see the price tag.

authors.name: Philip K. Dick
authors.biography: American visionary of unstable realities and counterfeit humans, scarcely solvent in life, now the most adapted science fiction writer in Hollywood.

books.title: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?
books.description: Bounty hunter Rick Deckard retires six escaped androids in a dust-choked San Francisco while wondering what, if anything, separates him from them.

books.title: The Man in the High Castle
books.description: In an America divided between victorious Japan and Germany, a banned novel describes a world where the Allies won — and may be more than fiction.

books.title: Ubik
books.description: After an ambush on the moon, Joe Chip's reality begins decaying backward through time, held together only by a spray can of Ubik.

authors.name: William Gibson
authors.biography: American-Canadian writer who coined 'cyberspace' and founded cyberpunk, then turned his pattern-recognition eye on the present.

books.title: Neuromancer
books.description: Burned-out hacker Case is hired for one last run against an AI's prison, jacked into the consensual hallucination of cyberspace, in the novel that invented a genre.

books.title: Pattern Recognition
books.description: Brand-allergic coolhunter Cayce Pollard chases anonymous film fragments across a post-9/11 world where marketing and meaning blur.

authors.name: Neal Stephenson
authors.biography: American author of maximalist speculative epics on code, money, language viruses, and civilizational reboot.

books.title: Snow Crash
books.description: Pizza-delivery hacker Hiro Protagonist and skateboard courier Y.T. chase a mind-crashing drug-religion through a franchised America and the Metaverse.

books.title: Cryptonomicon
books.description: WWII codebreakers and 1990s crypto-entrepreneurs braid into one saga of secrecy, gold, and the mathematics of information.

authors.name: Octavia E. Butler
authors.biography: Pioneering Black science fiction writer and MacArthur fellow whose unflinching novels of power, survival, and symbiosis grow only more prophetic.

books.title: Kindred
books.description: Dana, a Black writer in 1976 Los Angeles, is repeatedly yanked back to an antebellum Maryland plantation to save the white ancestor who enslaves her family.

books.title: Parable of the Sower
books.description: As 2020s California burns, hyperempathic teenager Lauren Olamina walks north with a new scripture: God is Change.

books.title: Dawn
books.description: Lilith wakes centuries after nuclear war aboard a living alien ship whose owners offer humanity survival — at the price of becoming something else.

authors.name: N.K. Jemisin
authors.biography: American author who won an unprecedented three consecutive Hugo Awards for a trilogy about a world that ends over and over.

books.title: The Fifth Season
books.description: On a continent wracked by apocalyptic Seasons, a woman whose earth-moving powers mark her for slavery hunts the husband who murdered their son.

books.title: The Obelisk Gate
books.description: Essun learns the moon can be caught and the Seasons ended, while her daughter Nassun learns the world might not deserve saving.

books.title: The Stone Sky
books.description: Mother and daughter converge at the world's wound with opposite intentions for the moon's return, in the trilogy's shattering conclusion.

authors.name: Liu Cixin
authors.biography: Chinese engineer and science fiction giant whose cosmic trilogy brought Chinese SF to world prominence and won the Hugo Award.

books.title: The Three-Body Problem
books.description: A secret signal sent during the Cultural Revolution draws an alien civilization from a dying triple-star system toward Earth — and some humans welcome them.

books.title: The Dark Forest
books.description: With Trisolaran fleets en route and human thought transparent to alien sophons, Earth's only hope lies with four Wallfacers whose plans must stay inside their heads.

books.title: Death's End
books.description: Engineer Cheng Xin inherits the deterrence that holds two civilizations at bay, in a finale that swallows dimensions and epochs whole.

authors.name: Andy Weir
authors.biography: Former software engineer whose self-published castaway-on-Mars story became a publishing and Hollywood phenomenon built on duct tape and arithmetic.

books.title: The Martian
books.description: Stranded astronaut Mark Watney sciences his way through potatoes, hexadecimal, and explosive decompression to survive alone on Mars.

books.title: Project Hail Mary
books.description: Ryland Grace wakes alone on a starship with amnesia, a dead crew, and a mission to save Earth's sun — and finds an unforgettable ally named Rocky.

books.title: Artemis
books.description: Smuggler Jazz Bashara takes one lucrative sabotage job in the moon's only city and ends up owning its biggest crisis.

authors.name: Martha Wells
authors.biography: American SFF veteran whose socially anxious, soap-opera-loving security android made her a multiple Hugo winner decades into her career.

books.title: All Systems Red
books.description: A self-hacked SecUnit that calls itself Murderbot would rather watch serials than protect its human clients — until someone starts killing them.

books.title: Network Effect
books.description: Murderbot's first full-length adventure: kidnapped clients, a hijacked research transport, and alien contamination it absolutely did not sign up for.

authors.name: J.R.R. Tolkien
authors.biography: Oxford philologist who built languages first and worlds to hold them, founding modern fantasy with Middle-earth.

books.title: The Hobbit
books.description: Comfort-loving Bilbo Baggins is swept out his round door by thirteen dwarves and a wizard to burgle a dragon's mountain hoard.

books.title: The Fellowship of the Ring
books.description: Frodo inherits a ring that must be destroyed in the fire where it was forged, and nine walkers set out from Rivendell against the Shadow.

books.title: The Two Towers
books.description: The broken Fellowship fights on two fronts as Frodo and Sam follow the creature Gollum toward Mordor's back door.

books.title: The Return of the King
books.description: Armies converge on Minas Tirith and two small hobbits crawl up Mount Doom carrying the fate of Middle-earth.

authors.name: C.S. Lewis
authors.biography: Belfast-born Oxford don, Christian apologist, and Tolkien's friend, whose wardrobe opened twentieth-century childhood onto Narnia.

books.title: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
books.description: Four evacuee children step through a wardrobe into Narnia, where it is always winter under the White Witch — until Aslan moves.

books.title: The Screwtape Letters
books.description: Senior demon Screwtape coaches his nephew Wormwood in the fine art of damning one ordinary man, in Lewis's wickedly inverted moral classic.

authors.name: George R.R. Martin
authors.biography: American author whose unsentimental epic of thrones and winter redefined fantasy's relationship to history and mortality.

books.title: A Game of Thrones
books.description: Ned Stark goes south to serve a king he loves among nobles who play the game of thrones, where you win or you die — and winter is coming.

books.title: A Clash of Kings
books.description: Four kings claim the Iron Throne as a red comet rises, and across the sea the last dragons wake.

authors.name: Patrick Rothfuss
authors.biography: American fantasist whose lyrical first novel about a legendary musician-arcanist set a generation of readers waiting for book three.

books.title: The Name of the Wind
books.description: In a wayside inn, the legend Kvothe tells the true story of his rise from orphaned trouper to youngest student of the University's hidden magic.

authors.name: Brandon Sanderson
authors.biography: Prolific American fantasist famed for rigorous magic systems, interconnected worlds, and finishing things — including Robert Jordan's Wheel of Time.

books.title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
books.description: Street thief Vin joins a crew of metal-burning Allomancers plotting to overthrow an immortal emperor who already won the prophecy a thousand years ago.

books.title: The Way of Kings
books.description: On storm-scoured Roshar, a broken soldier, a scholarly thief, and a doomed highprince converge on oaths that could rebind the world.

books.title: Elantris
books.description: The god-city Elantris has rotted, and its newest cursed resident, Prince Raoden, organizes the damned while his bride investigates above.

authors.name: Neil Gaiman
authors.biography: English author of dark contemporary myth across novels, comics, and film, where old gods ride buses and graveyards raise children.

books.title: American Gods
books.description: Ex-convict Shadow takes a job with the grifter god Wednesday as the old deities immigrants carried to America prepare for war with the new gods of media and tech.

books.title: The Graveyard Book
books.description: A toddler escapes his family's murderer and is raised by ghosts in a cemetery, where being dead is safer than the world of the living.

books.title: Coraline
books.description: Through a bricked-up door, Coraline finds an Other Mother with button eyes who wants her to stay forever.

books.title: Good Omens
books.description: An angel and a demon who rather like Earth team up to misplace the Antichrist and derail Armageddon, in Gaiman and Pratchett's beloved collaboration.

authors.name: Terry Pratchett
authors.biography: English satirist whose forty-one Discworld novels carried a flat world on four elephants and a turtle, and the sharpest moral comedy in fantasy.

books.title: Guards! Guards!
books.description: A dragon is summoned over Ankh-Morpork and the despised Night Watch — a drunk captain, a fraud, and a six-foot dwarf — must rise to the occasion.

books.title: Mort
books.description: Death takes an apprentice, and gangly farm boy Mort discovers that compassion plus a scythe equals catastrophic timeline damage.

books.title: Small Gods
books.description: The Great God Om manifests as a one-eyed tortoise to find only one true believer left, a novice with a perfect memory, in Pratchett's slyest book on belief.

authors.name: Robin Hobb
authors.biography: American fantasy author whose intimate first-person epics about the royal bastard FitzChivalry redefined character-driven fantasy.

books.title: Assassin's Apprentice
books.description: Royal bastard Fitz is trained in the quiet arts of the king's assassin while his forbidden bond with animals marks him for contempt.

authors.name: Naomi Novik
authors.biography: American author who moved from Napoleonic dragons to Eastern European fairy-tale retellings and a homicidal magic school.

books.title: Uprooted
books.description: Every ten years the wizard called the Dragon takes a village girl; this time he takes clumsy Agnieszka, whose untrained power may match the malevolent Wood.

books.title: A Deadly Education
books.description: At the Scholomance, where monsters eat students and there are no teachers, dark-affinity El must survive senior year without becoming the villain she's prophesied to be.

authors.name: V.E. Schwab
authors.biography: American author of inventive fantasy across adult and YA, from parallel Londons to a girl cursed to be forgotten.

books.title: The Invisible Life of Addie LaRue
books.description: In 1714 Addie trades her soul for freedom and is cursed to be forgotten by everyone she meets — until, three centuries later, a bookseller remembers her.

books.title: A Darker Shade of Magic
books.description: Kell, one of the last blood magicians who can walk between parallel Londons, smuggles a forbidden artifact that could wake the dead London no one names.

authors.name: Nnedi Okorafor
authors.biography: Nigerian American author of Africanfuturist fiction where ancient tradition and far-future technology share the same desert.

books.title: Binti
books.description: The first Himba girl admitted to a galactic university survives a massacre aboard her transport by becoming the bridge between species at war.

books.title: Who Fears Death
books.description: In post-apocalyptic Saharan Africa, sorceress Onyesonwu — 'who fears death' — is born of violence and prophesied to rewrite her people's fate.

authors.name: Agatha Christie
authors.biography: The bestselling novelist in history, creator of Hercule Poirot and Miss Marple, whose ingenious puzzles defined the golden age of detective fiction.

books.title: And Then There Were None
books.description: Ten strangers lured to an island are accused of murder by a gramophone record and then die, one by one, according to a nursery rhyme.

books.title: Murder on the Orient Express
books.description: A snowbound luxury train, a stabbed American tycoon, and twelve suspects give Hercule Poirot his most famous — and most morally vexing — case.

books.title: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd
books.description: Poirot comes out of retirement in a quiet village where everyone has secrets, in the mystery whose solution scandalized the genre.

books.title: Death on the Nile
books.description: A honeymoon cruise up the Nile turns deadly when the radiant heiress everyone envied is found shot, and Poirot is aboard.

books.title: The A.B.C. Murders
books.description: A killer working through the alphabet taunts Poirot by letter before each murder, leaving an ABC railway guide beside every body.

authors.name: Arthur Conan Doyle
authors.biography: Scottish physician who created the world's most famous detective and tried, unsuccessfully, to kill him.

books.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
books.description: Twelve cases from Baker Street, including 'A Scandal in Bohemia' and 'The Speckled Band,' that fixed the detective story's form forever.

books.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
books.description: A spectral hound stalks the Baskerville heirs across fog-bound Dartmoor, and Holmes must separate family curse from earthly murder.

books.title: A Study in Scarlet
books.description: Invalided army doctor John Watson takes rooms with an eccentric consulting detective, and a corpse in an empty Brixton house begins the legend.

authors.name: Raymond Chandler
authors.biography: Oil executive turned crime writer who gave hardboiled detective fiction its knight-errant and its style: wisecracks polished like ball bearings.

books.title: The Big Sleep
books.description: PI Philip Marlowe takes a blackmail case for a dying general and descends through Los Angeles's pornographers, gamblers, and killers.

books.title: The Long Goodbye
books.description: Marlowe's friendship with a polite drunk named Terry Lennox costs him jail time, beatings, and his illusions, in Chandler's most personal novel.

authors.name: Dashiell Hammett
authors.biography: Former Pinkerton operative who invented the hardboiled detective novel from the inside.

books.title: The Maltese Falcon
books.description: Sam Spade's partner is murdered chasing a jeweled bird worth killing for, and everyone who wants it lies — especially the lady.

authors.name: Patricia Highsmith
authors.biography: American novelist of elegant unease whose charming sociopaths made readers complicit in murder.

books.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
books.description: Sent to Italy to retrieve a rich man's son, Tom Ripley decides he'd rather become Dickie Greenleaf than fetch him.

books.title: Strangers on a Train
books.description: Two men meet on a train and one proposes swapping murders — a perfect crime that only works if both are mad enough to follow through.

authors.name: Stephen King
authors.biography: Maine's master of horror and the modern American gothic, author of more than sixty novels that made the bestseller list a haunted house.

books.title: The Shining
books.description: Recovering alcoholic Jack Torrance takes his family to winter-caretake the isolated Overlook Hotel, which has plans for his telepathic son — and for him.

books.title: It
books.description: Seven friends who defeated the shape-shifting evil beneath Derry as children are called home twenty-seven years later when It wakes hungry again.

books.title: Misery
books.description: Romance novelist Paul Sheldon wakes from a car crash in the remote home of his number-one fan, who has opinions about how his series should end.
A Maine teacher steps through a diner pantry into 1958 and spends five years preparing to stop the Kennedy assassination, while the past pushes back.

books.title: The Stand
books.description: A weaponized flu kills nearly everyone, and the survivors dream their way toward Mother Abagail in Boulder or the dark man in Las Vegas.

books.title: Pet Sematary
books.description: Behind the Creed family's new house lies a burial ground that brings things back — wrong — and grief makes Louis use it anyway.

authors.name: Shirley Jackson
authors.biography: American author of immaculate domestic dread, whose most famous story drew the angriest mail The New Yorker ever received.

books.title: The Haunting of Hill House
books.description: Four investigators move into a house that is not sane, and lonely Eleanor finds Hill House wants her most of all.

books.title: We Have Always Lived in the Castle
books.description: Merricat Blackwood guards her sister and their poisoned family's estate against a hostile village, until cousin Charles comes for the money.

authors.name: Bram Stoker
authors.biography: Irish theater manager whose epistolary vampire novel fixed the modern image of the undead aristocrat.

books.title: Dracula
books.description: Told in letters, diaries, and phonograph records, the Count's move from Transylvania to London is opposed by a band of friends led by Van Helsing.

authors.name: Mary Shelley
authors.biography: English Romantic who, at eighteen, invented science fiction during a wet summer on Lake Geneva.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.description: Victor Frankenstein animates a creature from dead matter and abandons it; the articulate, suffering being exacts a terrible education in responsibility.

authors.name: Oscar Wilde
authors.biography: Irish wit, playwright, and martyr to Victorian hypocrisy, whose epigrams outlived the society that jailed him.

books.title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
books.description: Beautiful Dorian stays young while his portrait ages and corrupts, recording every sin his face refuses to show.

books.title: The Importance of Being Earnest
books.description: Two bachelors invent fictitious identities to escape social duty, and the truth unravels over cucumber sandwiches, in the funniest play in English.

authors.name: Gillian Flynn
authors.biography: American crime writer and screenwriter whose unreliable narrators and 'cool girl' takedown reshaped the psychological thriller.

books.title: Gone Girl
books.description: Amy Dunne vanishes on her fifth wedding anniversary and her diary points to husband Nick — but neither spouse's story is what it seems.

books.title: Sharp Objects
books.description: Reporter Camille Preaker returns to her Missouri hometown to cover murdered girls and must excavate her own poisonous family to find the killer.

authors.name: Stieg Larsson
authors.biography: Swedish journalist and anti-fascist investigator whose posthumously published Millennium trilogy became a global publishing event.

books.title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
books.description: Disgraced journalist Mikael Blomkvist and brilliant, damaged hacker Lisbeth Salander hunt a forty-year-old disappearance through a wealthy family's rot.

books.title: The Girl Who Played with Fire
books.description: Salander is framed for three murders tied to a sex-trafficking exposé, and the secret that explains her past begins to surface.

authors.name: Tana French
authors.biography: American-born Irish writer whose Dublin Murder Squad novels turned the police procedural into psychological literature.

books.title: In the Woods
books.description: Detective Rob Ryan investigates a child's murder in the same woods where his own childhood friends vanished — a case he survived but cannot remember.

books.title: The Likeness
books.description: Detective Cassie Maddox goes undercover as a murdered woman who was living under Cassie's own abandoned alias, inside the dead girl's strange household.

authors.name: John le Carré
authors.biography: Former British intelligence officer who elevated the spy novel into moral literature about betrayal, bureaucracy, and the Cold War's human cost.

books.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy
books.description: Forced-out spymaster George Smiley is recalled to find the Soviet mole at the very top of the Circus, sifting decades of friendship for the lie.

books.title: The Spy Who Came in from the Cold
books.description: Burned-out agent Alec Leamas plays one last double game in East Germany, where the line between his side and theirs dissolves.

authors.name: Frederick Forsyth
authors.biography: RAF pilot and foreign correspondent who invented the modern documentary thriller with journalistic precision.

books.title: The Day of the Jackal
books.description: An anonymous English assassin codenamed Jackal is hired to kill Charles de Gaulle, and a French detective has weeks to find a man who doesn't exist.

authors.name: Lee Child
authors.biography: British television director who, laid off at forty, created the wandering ex-MP giant Jack Reacher and one of thriller fiction's great franchises.

books.title: Killing Floor
books.description: Drifter Jack Reacher steps off a bus in Margrave, Georgia, and is arrested for murder within hours — and the victim turns out to be family.

authors.name: Michael Connelly
authors.biography: Former crime-beat reporter whose LAPD detective Harry Bosch carries the motto: everybody counts or nobody counts.

books.title: The Lincoln Lawyer
books.description: Defense attorney Mickey Haller works LA from the back of a Lincoln until a rich client's case turns out to connect to the one innocent man he failed.

authors.name: James Patterson
authors.biography: Former ad executive and the world's most commercially successful living author, famed for propulsive chapters and the Alex Cross series.

books.title: Along Came a Spider
books.description: DC detective-psychologist Alex Cross hunts a schoolteacher kidnapper who wants to commit the crime of the century.

authors.name: Dan Brown
authors.biography: American thriller writer whose symbologist hero turned art history and conspiracy into airport-conquering blockbusters.

books.title: The Da Vinci Code
books.description: A murder in the Louvre plunges Robert Langdon into a cipher-strewn hunt for the Holy Grail and a secret the Church spent centuries guarding.

books.title: Angels & Demons
books.description: Langdon races an Illuminati plot through Rome's churches as antimatter stolen from CERN counts down beneath the Vatican.

authors.name: Paula Hawkins
authors.biography: Zimbabwe-born British journalist whose commuter-window thriller became one of the fastest-selling adult novels in history.

books.title: The Girl on the Train
books.description: Blackout drinker Rachel watches a perfect couple from her commuter train until the wife vanishes — during a night Rachel can't remember.

authors.name: Alex Michaelides
authors.biography: Cypriot-British screenwriter whose debut psychological thriller, built on a Greek tragedy, sold copies in the millions.

books.title: The Silent Patient
books.description: Painter Alicia Berenson shoots her husband and never speaks again; psychotherapist Theo Faber is determined to make her talk.

authors.name: J.K. Rowling
authors.biography: British author whose boy wizard, conceived on a delayed train, became the bestselling book series in history.

books.title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
books.description: Orphan Harry Potter learns on his eleventh birthday that he is a wizard, famous for surviving the dark lord who killed his parents.

books.title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
books.description: Something is petrifying students at Hogwarts, a voice whispers in the walls, and a fifty-year-old diary remembers things differently.

books.title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
books.description: Escaped convict Sirius Black is coming for Harry — say the headlines — while dementors drain joy from Hogwarts and a time-turner waits.

authors.name: Suzanne Collins
authors.biography: American television writer whose televised-death-match trilogy ignited the YA dystopia boom.

books.title: The Hunger Games
books.description: Katniss Everdeen volunteers for her sister in the Capitol's annual fight-to-the-death broadcast and becomes a spark the regime can't control.

books.title: Catching Fire
books.description: Katniss's victory tour fans rebellion, and the seventy-fifth Games reap their tributes from the surviving victors themselves.

authors.name: John Green
authors.biography: American YA author and video blogger whose articulate teenagers face mortality, anxiety, and first love with quotable grace.

books.title: The Fault in Our Stars
books.description: Hazel and Augustus meet in a cancer support group and fall in love over a novel with no ending, traveling to Amsterdam to demand one.

books.title: Looking for Alaska
books.description: Miles 'Pudge' Halter goes to boarding school seeking a Great Perhaps and finds it in brilliant, self-destructing Alaska Young.

authors.name: Angie Thomas
authors.biography: American YA novelist from Jackson, Mississippi, whose debut about police violence became a defining book for a generation.

books.title: The Hate U Give
books.description: Starr Carter is the only witness when a cop kills her unarmed friend Khalil, and her two worlds — prep school and her neighborhood — collide.

authors.name: Rick Riordan
authors.biography: Former middle-school teacher who turned Greek mythology into bedtime contraband for millions of kids.

books.title: The Lightning Thief
books.description: ADHD sixth-grader Percy Jackson learns he's Poseidon's son and has ten days to find Zeus's stolen lightning bolt before the gods go to war.

books.title: The Sea of Monsters
books.description: Percy sails the monster-filled Sea of Monsters to rescue his friend Grover and the poisoned camp's only hope, the Golden Fleece.

authors.name: Roald Dahl
authors.biography: Welsh-born RAF pilot turned beloved children's author of gleefully wicked tales where horrid adults get exactly what they deserve.

books.title: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
books.description: Poor Charlie Bucket finds the last Golden Ticket and tours Willy Wonka's factory as the other winners are eliminated by their own vices.

books.title: Matilda
books.description: A genius five-year-old with telekinetic powers and terrible parents takes on the child-hurling headmistress Miss Trunchbull.

books.title: James and the Giant Peach
books.description: Orphan James escapes his dreadful aunts inside an enormous peach, crewed by human-sized insects, across the Atlantic.

books.title: The BFG
books.description: Sophie is snatched from her orphanage by the Big Friendly Giant, the only giant who doesn't eat children, and together they plot to stop the ones who do.

authors.name: E.B. White
authors.biography: New Yorker essayist and prose stylist who wrote three of the most loved children's books in American literature.

books.title: Charlotte's Web
books.description: A spider named Charlotte spins words into her web to save the runt pig Wilbur from slaughter, in the great American story of friendship and loss.

authors.name: Madeleine L'Engle
authors.biography: American writer who folded quantum physics and theology into children's fiction, rejected twenty-six times before winning the Newbery Medal.

books.title: A Wrinkle in Time
books.description: Meg Murry, her uncanny little brother, and three celestial ladies tesser across space to rescue her father from a planet ruled by a disembodied brain.

authors.name: Lois Lowry
authors.biography: Two-time Newbery medalist whose quiet dystopia about a community without pain or color became required reading.

books.title: The Giver
books.description: In a community that has traded memory and feeling for sameness, twelve-year-old Jonas is chosen to receive everything that was given up.

books.title: Number the Stars
books.description: Ten-year-old Annemarie helps smuggle her Jewish best friend's family from occupied Copenhagen to Sweden in 1943.

authors.name: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
authors.biography: French aviator-author lost over the Mediterranean in WWII, whose desert fable remains among the most translated books ever written.

books.title: The Little Prince
books.description: A pilot downed in the Sahara meets a golden-haired prince from asteroid B-612, who reports what grown-ups have forgotten: what is essential is invisible to the eye.

authors.name: Lewis Carroll
authors.biography: Oxford mathematician Charles Dodgson, whose boat-trip story for a young friend became the masterpiece of literary nonsense.

books.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
books.description: Alice follows a waistcoated White Rabbit down a hole into a world of mad tea parties, croquet-playing queens, and logic turned inside out.

authors.name: L.M. Montgomery
authors.biography: Canadian novelist whose red-haired orphan put Prince Edward Island on the world's literary map.

books.title: Anne of Green Gables
books.description: Elderly siblings send for an orphan boy to help on the farm and receive instead Anne Shirley — talkative, dramatic, and impossible not to love.

authors.name: Louisa May Alcott
authors.biography: Massachusetts transcendentalist's daughter who wrote her family into the most enduring American girlhood story.

books.title: Little Women
books.description: Meg, Jo, Beth, and Amy March grow up genteel-poor in Civil War New England, anchored by their mother and Jo's stubborn ambition to write.

authors.name: Frances Hodgson Burnett
authors.biography: English-American author whose tales of transformed, transplanted children became nursery-shelf immortals.

books.title: The Secret Garden
books.description: Sour orphan Mary Lennox discovers a locked garden on a Yorkshire moor estate, and bringing it back to life heals more than the roses.

authors.name: A.A. Milne
authors.biography: English playwright whose stories for his son Christopher Robin gave the world its most beloved bear.

books.title: Winnie-the-Pooh
books.description: A bear of very little brain, a gloomy donkey, and a small pig in a windy wood — adventures of honey, heffalumps, and friendship in the Hundred Acre Wood.

authors.name: Katherine Paterson
authors.biography: American children's author and two-time Newbery medalist who trusts young readers with grief.

books.title: Bridge to Terabithia
books.description: Jess and Leslie rule an imaginary kingdom across the creek until a rainy morning changes everything, in a classic of friendship and loss.

authors.name: Stephen Chbosky
authors.biography: American novelist and filmmaker whose epistolary debut became a rite of passage passed hand to hand through high schools.

books.title: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
books.description: Freshman Charlie writes letters to a stranger about mixtapes, first love, found friends, and the memory he's been protecting himself from.

authors.name: Veronica Roth
authors.biography: American author who sold her faction-divided dystopia while still in college and topped bestseller lists before twenty-five.

books.title: Divergent
books.description: In faction-divided Chicago, Tris Prior tests as Divergent — fitting no faction — a secret that could get her killed.

authors.name: Tomi Adeyemi
authors.biography: Nigerian American novelist whose West African-inspired fantasy debut landed a record-setting deal and a devoted readership.

books.title: Children of Blood and Bone
books.description: Zélie has one chance to restore magic to Orïsha and strike against the monarchy that slaughtered the maji — including her mother.
